title: Charred Shaggy Northern-Thai Fish Ttukbaegi
description: Crusty, flavorful fish cooked in an earthenware ttukbaegi pot with shaggy, post-workout vibes. This lacto-vegetarian-friendly dish is perfect for a quick weeknight dinner.

ingredients.name: Tra fish fillets (about 4 oz)
ingredients.notes: tra fish or other firm white fish works well

instructions: Season the tra fish fillets with a pinch of salt and pepper. Place them in the bottom of a ttukbaegi pot or a shallow baking dish.
In a blender or food processor, combine the Thai chilies, galangal or Thai ginger, garlic, and shallots. Blend until you get a rough paste.
Pour the coconut milk into the blender and blend until well combined with the spice paste.
Pour the spice paste mixture over the fish fillets in the pot. Add the fish sauce, sugar, and lime juice, and stir to combine.
Transfer the pot to the preheated oven and cook for 10-12 minutes, or until the fish is cooked through and the top is charred.
Remove the pot from the oven and carefully flip the fish fillets over.
Return the pot to the oven and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, or until the other side is also charred.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it cool slightly.
Serve the charred fish fillets in the ttukbaegi pot, garnished with fresh cilantro or basil leaves if desired.
Serve with a side of steamed jasmine rice or noodles.
Garnish with additional Thai chilies, lime wedges, or cilantro if desired.
